no debris, but many roots emerge everywhere and sometimes out of place, as if on
purpose. North and west from here, the forest is still odd and dark and it might
not be a pleasant place to travel. The passages south and east, where the forest
is greener and at least more normal, if not welcoming, are the easiest.
name: Dark Tangled Forest

desc:
As the ground rises here, the trees thin slightly. Through a small gap in the
trees to the west, the dim outline of a mountain range can be made out on the
horizon, many days of travelling away. Short grass carpets the ground, covering
the tree roots that have erupted above ground. To the south the trees stretch
along a steep rise.
name: A Rising Forest

desc:
If it were not for the unbroken canopy blocking the view of the valley below,
the top of this hill would make an excellent vantage point from which one could
observe movement in the forest. To the east the hill slopes steeply away.
name: A Forested Ridge

desc:
The slope to the eastern side is littered with rocks of various shapes and
sizes. Worn by years of exposure to wind and rain the rocks have become smooth
and even, their unprotected backs polished by weather and time. To the east the
ground slopes away while the hills continue to the north and south.
name: Along a Forested Ridge

desc:
The ridge runs north-south and the forest follows it unbroken. Roots from the
old trees stretch far from the trunks, occasionally breaking clear above the
ground, presenting a great hazard for the careless or unwary traveller.
name: A Tree-lined Ridge

desc:
Climbing steadily upwards, the forest rises away from the valley to the south-
east. The long slope is clad with grass beneath the tall evergreens that creak
softly as the wind plays through their branches. From the vantage point here,
high mountains can be seen towering to the west. It would take many days of
travel to reach their base.
name: The Southern Edge of a Ridge

desc:
While the valley opens out to the northeast, further south and west the forest
becomes increasing dense. The ground here is cold, unnaturally cold. Nothing
grows near the base of the trees save some of the hardier varieties of moss. The
leaves on the branches above make a soft rustling sound as they sway in the
gentle breeze.
name: Approaching a Dense Forest

desc:
Entering this part of the forest is no simple matter. Thick tree roots snake
their way across the forest floor and strong vines hug the trees as if trying to
strangle the life out of them. It is noticeably darker here than in the area to
the north. Besides that, the cool air and silence creates an eerie, almost
oppressive atmosphere.
name: Dark and Tangled Forest

desc:
Great dark trees grow in a line on either side of a small stream. Gnarled boughs
betray the age of the ancient trees. The stream itself is almost the same colour
as the bark on the trees. The soft earth surrounding the water being slowly
eroded over time. A wise traveller would do well to avoid drinking from such a
polluted source. Looking further downstream, little can be seen save a shallow
gully to the east.
name: Among the Gnarled Trees

desc:
A thin trickle of water runs across the ground making the area very boggy and
the air damp and humid. A musty smell prevails and since the canopy of leaves
still arches above, evidently sheltered from the wind, there is little chance of
fresh air here. Further north, the sound of rustling leaves can be heard. To the
south the ground slopes gently away, leading to the bottom of the valley. A
small hill flanks the valley to the west, clearly the source of the water that
drains into this area.
name: Dimly Lit Gully

desc:
To the south the willow trees at the tunnel mouth frame the woodland beyond in
the shape of an arch. The tunnel walls are constructed of small twigs woven into
solid panels that confine movement. To the north and east the tunnel seems to
lead away, deeper into the forest. The only obvious exit is the southern path
stretching into the forest.
name: A Thatched Tunnel

desc:
The tunnel comes to an abrupt end, the body of a giant willow tree forms the
eastern wall. Supple ash trees growing in straight rows have been bent together
and fixed in place to form a natural canopy overhead. The branches high up have
woven together over time, casting the area into darkness save for a few narrow
shafts of light that pierce the gloom only when the sun is directly overhead.
name: End of a Thatched Tunnel

desc:
The forest seems to stir as if to direct wanderers to a weeping willow. Standing
forty feet high it is a magnificent specimen. The parasol of leaves and branches
reaching the ground some twenty paces from the trunk, creating a haven of
relaxation underneath. The bark of the tree is covered with patches of white
lichen giving it a slightly unnatural, mottled effect. The remnants of an
ancient track can just be made out winding through the trees. The path goes no
further west than the willow.
name: Willow Grove

desc:
In stark contrast to the rest of the forest, the trees growing here appear to be
quite young. Their thin trunks are covered with a rich dark-brown bark, their
branches waving in the breeze, the movement of the leaves creating waves of
noise. Around the base of each willow a carpet of grass has sprung up. Only the
thin, brown line of a small path leading to the west spoils the uniformity.
name: Among the Willows

desc:
The southern end of the wide path between the birch trees opens out to reveal a
thicket of imposing greenery further to the south. The path turns and heads to
the west but only continues a few yards before becoming nothing more than an
overgrown track. On the eastern edge of the opening, amid the surviving trees,
several tree stumps lie covered with ferns.
name: A Tree-lined Avenue

desc:
These pale birch trees appear to glow in the dim light. The silver bark has few
blemishes and stands in stark contrast to the remainder of the drab forest.
Insects crawl back and forth, busily attending to their tasks, among the moss
and fungi growing between the roots.
name: Ghostly, White Trees

desc:
The forest becomes increasingly tangled as the numerous branches grow closer and
closer together. They almost seem to have a mind of their own as they appear to
actively inhibit movement progress. To the west a break in the dense forest
appears, at least the woodland seems lighter in that direction. Further to the
south the ground levels off as the top of the hill is reached.
name: Rising Forest

desc:
On all sides, there are dense trees interspersed with vines and rock piles. The
trees are small, almost stunted, as they try to force their roots deep into the
wet ground looking for nutrients. The damp earth feels extremely soft underfoot
and a stench of decay fills the air. The thickest parts of the forest lie to the
east, west, and north. Only the rising ground to the south offers any change in
surroundings.
name: Cold, Cheerless Hollow

desc:
A sense of stillness and calm fills this area. The trees here are not troubled
by any wind so the leaves and branches remain motionless and silent. The ferns
growing in the thicket provide a burst of greenery in contrast to the dark
matting of dead leaves covering the ground. To the west the forest thins
slightly while the closely packed trees stretch as far as the eye can see in
other directions.
name: Silent Thicket

desc:
A group of tree stumps dominate the centre of the path. The stumps vary quite
dramatically in girth. The smallest is less than seven inches across while the
largest measures two feet. On each of the stumps a number of axe marks can be
clearly seen. The cuts do not appear to lie in neat lines, whoever cut these
trees either did so in a hurry or lacked the skill to fell them with more care.
Around these stumps several deep furrows have been made in the soft earth. The
furrows lie along the line of the avenue as if heavy items had once been dragged
along here.
name: A Tree-lined Avenue

desc:
This is the fringe of a lighter, less populated part of the forest. To the east
a number of trees can be seen clustered together as if in a regimented line. The
fragrance of fresh flowers and herbs drifts in from the north.
name: Sparse Forest

desc:
The land here is flat and open, gorse bushes and shrubs outnumber the trees that
are struggling to grow in the sandy soil. There is little to distract the eye
from the monotony of stunted trees. To the north the trees thicken slightly.
name: Scrub Forest

desc:
The land here is far less fertile than in the forest to the north. The trees are
smaller and more widely spaced, allowing much more sunlight to reach the forest
floor. Scattered around are several piles of dried brushwood and leaves. With
each footstep upon the ground a loud cracking sound can be heard.
name: Scrub Forest

desc:
The tunnel ceiling is barely six feet high in the centre, taller travellers are
forced to stoop in order to gain access to the darker recesses. Very little
light reaches this place, making it difficult to see exactly how the chamber was
created. What is obvious is that it is made entirely of natural materials, as if
the trees had been bent and forced to grow into certain shapes.
name: A Thatched Tunnel

desc:
The trees crowd around the valley sides, blocking any sunlight from reaching the
depths of the valley far below. Movement here is difficult as the sheer number
of roots and fallen branches impede progress. Climbing out of the valley to the
east looks to be impossible, a solid wall of wood blocks any movement that way.
To the south the gully continues.
name: Dimly Lit Gully

desc:
As the valley opens out to the south, the same kind of plain trees that populate
this area can be seen far to the south. While the surroundings does not offer
any spectacular botanic impressions, the near complete lack of undergrowth makes
way for unhindered movement.
name: Shrouded Valley

desc:
Tall, straight, silver-bark trees, reminiscent of the ash, grow at the foot of a
hill. Off to the west, scattered groups of trees can be seen lining the ridge as
it climbs towards the horizon. In the evening these trees block the light as the
sun sets, casting this small grove into a premature grey dusk.
name: A Small Grove

desc:
As the ground slopes gently upwards, the predominance of rowan trees gives way
to hardy evergreens. At the base of the trees, the twigs stretch out as if
searching for the warm sunlight. Further up the slope to the west, the ground
becomes increasingly stony.
name: Rising Ground

desc:
This area of the forest is populated almost entirely by rowan trees. One tree in
particular stands out, at thirty feet high, it stands tall above the smaller
ones that surround it. To the west a rocky slope leads up to a ridge while to
the east the forest becomes noticeably thicker.
name: Grove of Rowan Trees

desc:
The trees here tower high above. One of the older ones has branches drooping low
to the ground, and the number of ants and beetles emerging from within its trunk
indicates that it may be rotten. The forest continues in all directions.
name: Bottom of a Forested Hill

desc:
Amid the boggy ground several clumps of trees can be seen. The forest is less
dense here and further to the east the trees completely vanish, resulting in a
clearing. A large number of flowers grow here, giving the area a peculiar odour.
name: A Wooded Knoll

desc:
Fangorn is surrounding this clearing that is composed of little more than some
grass and brushwood. An excessive amount of water has caused a flooding in the
western edge of the clearing. The water emerging from the soggy, saturated
ground is brown and has a musty but sharp smell.
name: A Clearing Deep within the Forest

desc:
Very little light enters here. The branches of neighbouring trees have grown
together, creating a natural ceiling and casting the forest floor into darkness.
Some ivy is making a vain attempt to grow far enough to reach what little
sunlight may penetrate the forest canopy.
name: Tangled Forest

desc:
Ancient oak trees stand here, their trunks penetrating the forest canopy and
their roots burrowing deep into the earth. These oaks have clearly grown here
for many decades, perhaps even hundreds of years. Around the base of these
magnificent trees a kind of dark, glossy shrub, resembling a type of thornless
holly, clings to the trunks.
name: Majestic Oak Trees

desc:
There is a very noticeable change in the forest here. To the east, the trees
grow close, their high-reaching branches knotting and twisting together to form
a kind of wooden ceiling. In years long past, the forest there would have been
glorious, supporting varieties of wildlife now gone from the Fangorn forever.
Further to the west, the trees have fared better. Their old boughs supporting
many leafy branches, however, there is a sense of unhappiness that cannot be
fully explained. It is almost as if the trees are grieving for the loss of the
Entwives all those years ago.
name: Border of a Solid Canopy

desc:
A wall of tangled roots blocks the way to the north. It would take a large group
of skilled woodsmen many days to clear a path through the twisted wood. An
easier path presents itself to the west and south. The forest to the east
appears dark. Forcing a path between the dreary, almost lifeless, rowan trees
will be tiring work.
name: Dense Black Forest

desc:
The lack of natural light reaching through the thick mass of branches growing
across the ceiling, prevents the growth of anything save the lichen clinging to
the drab and cheerless trees. The forest floor is littered with debris, broken
twigs and dry branches. Clearly, nothing has disturbed them for many years.
name: Cheerless Forest

desc:
All around, the view is blocked by the darkened trunks of trees. Branches grow
thick and heavily laden with foliage forming a natural barrier blocking out the
rays of the sun. It is cold here and the silence creates a sombre atmosphere. It
is obvious that very little happens here. The trees are bounded on all sides by
more dark and uninviting thickets.
name: Enshrouded in Darkness

desc:
The surrounding forest now seems to be getting lighter. Through the trees, it is
possible to see something of what is beyond them, however, the forest is still
dark enough not to reveal all its secrets. From deep within the forest, to the
south, a rustling sound can be heard, but nothing save more trees is to be seen.
name: Surrounded by Evergreens

desc:
Fir and rowan trees jostle for position. The smaller rowans are concentrated
mainly on the western side of the dip leaving the firs to spread their roots on
the eastern edge. A feeble trickle of water meanders its way around the boughs
feeding little more than few drips a minute into a large puddle in the centre of
the depression. The fragrance of the two varieties of tree fights with the smell
emanating from the stagnant water.
name: A Small Depression

desc:
Standing here, it becomes apparent that the area is cold because the dense
canopy of leaves above blocks out nearly all of the sunlight. What little light
does manage to pierce the barrier casts long eerie shadows. Wildlife does not
seem to favour this place, there is little evidence of any creatures living in
the trees here.
name: Cold Eerie Forest

desc:
The forest surrounding this area has grown taller and appears more densely
packed than the trees that stand here. At the base of several trees grow a
variety of fungi and lichen. They seem to be taking advantage of the increased
sunlight this area enjoys during the day.
name: A Copse

desc:
The forest stops abruptly to the north. The distinctive glint of still water can
just be made out in the clearing to the north, but nothing else is remarkable.
On the ground, numerous insects are at work slowly cutting, moving and eating
the dead leaves that lie about.
name: South of a Clearing

desc:
The trees here surround a natural hollow within which the remains of an old camp
can be seen. It seems that whoever rested here did not wait very long as they do
not appear to have made a fire. Around the edge of the hollow a number of small
flowers carpet the ground. Looking further, the forest stretches as far as the
eye can see in all directions.
name: Deep within the Forest

desc:
Under the sprawling branches of this large oak tree the air is cool. The leaves
of the tree are sheltering the ground from the sun and create a soft green
shadow. The ground is blanketed with thick grass and the occasional flowering
shrub giving the area a fresh, airy feeling despite the lack of natural light.
name: In the Shade of an Oak

desc:
There is a faint scent lingering here. A smell of fresh grass, a reminder of
summer. The trees here are younger than most in this forest, the usual dimness
of the deep forest does not permeate here. Instead, a soft green glow bathes the
area in a cold light during the day and at night the moon makes each tree shine
as it plays its silver beams across the bark.
name: A Gathering of Oak Trees

desc:
Flanked by a pair of towering willow trees, the path to the south leads into a
dark tunnel. The tunnel is made out of living wood, the branches of the willow
trees intertwining themselves to provide shelter. Looking deeper into the
tunnel, an aisle of trees can barely be discerned amid the gloom. How much
further this passage extends cannot be guessed at.
name: Before a Woodland Tunnel

desc:
Each footstep is muffled by the mat of rotting leaves that covers the dark
forest floor. A smell of decay hangs in the air, overpowering the scent of the
flowers that are growing in the rich soil. To the south and east the forest gets
lighter, and thus allowing for at least a little light to enter.
name: Carpet of Rotting Leaves

desc:
Fallen trees restrict movement on all sides. The only gap visible in the dense
woodland is to the north. On all sides the towering pine trees give the area an
oppressive feeling, standing tall and mighty, they unremittingly watch over the
forest and its visitors.
name: Impenetrable Forest

desc:
The trees here are so huge, their uppermost branches appear to reach into the
clouds. The view around is blocked by the large number of trunks that have grown
close together. To the south the forest is but a dark shape, very little can be
seen in that direction.
name: Among Tall Trees

desc:
The sky above the forest roof is completely obscured from sight, only the leaves
and branches of the surrounding trees can be seen. Glancing over the southern
trees, trying to discern what lies in the shadows, the branches shift slightly.
name: Deep within the Forest

desc:
The trees here have a rich, deep black bark closely resembling polished leather.
In the centre of this area the trees seem to cluster together as if for warmth
or protection. Passing between them there is a sense of stifling that leaves as
quickly as it came. Even at the outer edge of the trees an uncomfortable feeling
of being watched remains.
name: A Clump of Oak Trees

desc:
Sturdy oak trees have put their roots deep into the soil and now stand here
proud and tall. Around the base of the trees, a few crushed stems indicate that
some wildlife may have rested here recently, however, there is no sign of it
now. Looking further into the forest, paths lead off to the north, west and
south.
name: A Dusky Thicket

desc:
The forest here is old, very old. Several trees have fallen down, and their
trunks lie here, the size of them testament to the long life that the trees have
led. Small termites are at work burrowing into the dead wood. Despite the number
of old fallen trees, this part of the forest is certainly not dying. Dotted
around are many young saplings, being much smaller than the old trees they are
growing to replace, the forest appears more open here than elsewhere. In a few
more years this area will have regained much of its former glory.
name: Moss Covered Trunks

desc:
The trees that grow here are much less mature than the others in the forest.
Whereas the older trees jostle for position, the young saplings that grow here
enjoy a great deal of space. Further south the forest appears to be stunted
while to the east the trees stop completely.
name: Thinly Scattered Forest

desc:
The forest gives way to an area of open, fertile ground. Many different types of
plants grow here as if it was once a well-tended herb garden. The fragrance of
fresh lavender and clover fills the air, a refreshing change from some of the
other smells in the forest. Surrounding the clearing on all sides, Fangorn
stretches out into the distance.
name: A Flower-filled Glade

desc:
Dominating the landscape here is a graceful archway, formed by intertwining
branches, leading to the north. It is hard to make out what lies in the darkness
there. However, the grandeur of the entrance suggests that it was once an
important place. In recent times the arch has fallen into disrepair, weeds and
dead twigs have piled up to block the entrance. It would take some work to clear
a path large enough to squeeze through.
name: A Natural Archway

desc:
Massive oak trees have taken over this area of the forest. The dry ground is
covered with roots, twisting and turning as they seek out fertile soil. Each
tree has a light, hairy covering, presumably some form of moss. On the eastern
edge of the oak grove a huge pile of dead branches and brushwood has collected.
Further south the ground is littered with rocks and boulders.
name: Dark Oaks

desc:
Standing tall as if on guard duty, old hoary oak trees are clustered together
here, their large buttress roots reaching deep into the earth. Underfoot, a
blanket of crisp, dry leaves and acorns amplifies the noise of every footstep.
The covering appears to be relatively undisturbed indicating that there is
little traffic through this part of the forest. There is no sign of any living
creature, even the soil appears to be dusty and infertile. Only the fungal
growths on some of the older trees seem to thrive in these conditions. To the
south, a wall of solid wood prevents any further travel.
name: Surrounded by Dark Oaks

desc:
Many rowan trees stand here, reaching only fourteen feet into the air, the lower
branches droop low to the ground impeding the passage of all but the smallest.
Clinging to the branches are a variety of leaves, some with a healthy rich green
colour and others appearing a shade of dull yellow, dead and withered. All
around, larger trees encircle this quiet woodland.
name: Damp, Sombre Woodland

desc:
Densely packed, towering fir trees grow here, leaving little room for the the
younger rowans to grow. The lower branches of the firs are covered with dry,
brown needles, and small twigs carpet the ground, muffling the sound of
footfalls. A few slugs try to survive, clinging to the dark areas under stones.
name: Smothering Fir Trees

desc:
The ground here slopes down to what looks like an earthen bowl to the south.
While traversable, each step must be carefully planned in order for one not to
lose their footing. A thick wall of huge trees and ferns stand on the eastern
side of the incline, with a bit of effort however, it is possible force a path
that way.
name: Sloping Ground

desc:
At the base of the depression, the ground opens out into a large bowl shape. In
the centre a small group of oaks are clustered together, a thin mist swirling
around their base. Vines have covered some of the trees, they have become thick
enough to make it impossible to see through the gaps between adjacent trees.
name: Haunting Bowl

desc:
All is silent below the canopy, and no single leaf or branch moves. The air is
still and smells of decay. The rowans come to an abrupt end at the sloping
depression to the east but ancient oaks and pines take over, filling the space
and continuing the forest without changing its shape.
name: A Stand of Rowan Trees

desc:
Standing at the foot of this tree, one could be overwhelmed by its sheer size.
It would take nearly thirty man-sized paces to encircle it at the base. The bark
of the tree bears a number of odd marks, which seem unlikely to have been made
by nature. While the true cause of the scarring cannot be determined, it is
certain that this tree would have its fair share of stories to tell. Around the
base of the tree great clumps of grass grow up among the roots.
name: Ancient, Gnarled Oak

desc:
Two lines of silver-bark birch trees flank a wide avenue in the forest. Filling
the space, a bed of soft green grass has grown. Here and there a few tree stumps
erupt through the sea of green indicating that trees have once stood where there
is now just cold empty space. To the north an impressive oak tree can be seen,
framed in the gap between the birches.
name: A Tree-lined Avenue

desc:
Rising from the bogs and mire, these rounded knolls are densely blanketed with a
multitude of fragrant evergreens. Despite the poor soil they are growing in, the
trees have prospered, their massive boughs supporting a network of branches far
above. Under the protective canopy some frail looking green ferns struggle to
push through the layer of pine needles.
name: Amid Noble Evergreens

desc:
Small, dried fallen branches and pine needles crackle underneath the feet of
travellers throughout this dense forest of huge trees. Above, the branches are
so intertwined that nearly no light filters through them. Despite the dim light
and entangled trees, the grove carries the fresh smell of pine resin, a change
from the usual musty air. To the south, the forest becomes more dense and
gloomy.
name: Pine Grove

desc:
The carpet of leaves that covers the ground conceals damp, black soil. No
smaller vegetation, other than mosses and lichens, has managed to exist here.
The forest is silent save for the occasional rustling of leaves as the wind
catches them. Despite the silent, peaceful appearance, the presence of something
else can be distinguished, of something moving in the shadows.
name: Deep inside Fangorn Forest

desc:
As the forest climbs the slope, the pine trees seem to grow in stature too.
Thick brown bark covers the trunks, the age of the trees given away by the
number of long, sticky trails of sap clinging to the massive trunks. The smell
of pine is overpowering, a refreshing change to the usual damp, musty air in the
rest of the forest. Dark shapes are moving slowly among the trees off to the
east.
name: Rising Pines

desc:
The gap in the trees runs north-south from here. Bordering the area are birch
trees, standing in neat rows as if part of some army guarding over Fangorn. The
bark of the trees shines in the pale light admitted through the break in the
forest canopy. Clumps of ferns surround the base of the nearest trees and yet
traces of stale, musty air remain, overpowering the scent of growing plants.
name: A Tree-lined Avenue

desc:
This once noble willow tree bears many scars. It has been hollowed out by crude
tools many years ago. Whoever did this was certainly not a skilled carpenter,
sharp slivers still protrude from the wood. Here and there it is almost possible
to make out symbols carved in the wall. A whisker of lichen near the floor is
the only sign of life that can be found.
name: Inside a Huge Willow

desc:
In the centre of the room, a great stone table stands six feet high. There are
no chairs to be seen here, presumably the Ents had no need for them. Several
tufts of grass are scattered on the floor. Looking around the rest of the home,
it seems a few rays of light enter during the day through the eastern wall,
drawing attention to the dilapidated state of the place.
name: An Abandoned Ent-home

desc:
Water once used to flow here, between the granite and limestone layers. All that
is left now is a succession of large dry pots, sculpted in the tender limestone.
Still, if one listens carefully, it is possible to hear the rumbling noise of
water flowing somewhere, on a deeper level. North, the cave seems to come to an
end, in a star-shaped room where an icy breeze whirls as if it had been misled
there and was seeking an exit.
name: Dried Up Stream

desc:
This almost flat cave ends here in front of a wall of moss. The brook which used
to spring from here is gone, flowing deeper underground. In this part of the
cave, draperies of whitish diaphanous stalactites cover the walls, often
obstructing or concealing the way. Deeper west, the rock above changes to a dark
grey, and going further becomes difficult and hazardous, in the middle of this
chaos of crystalline rocks.
name: Underground Brook

desc:
The strong branches of the great trees end in front of the wall of a steep cliff
rising above the tree tops. An arrowtip-shaped piece of the cliff has collapsed
down in the forest, and the resulting breach is now at the height of the tree
forks. A large branch is only two feet away from the cliff and could be used to
jump into the breach. The way down is more hazardous, as no lower branches can
be spotted from this location.
name: On High Branches

desc:
Unlike the upper crypts, the sacrificial chambers are lavishly designed. The
walls are constructed of black marble with inlaid gold and silver engravings
displaying the extraction of souls from live victims. In one mosaic the victim
can be seen contorted in agony as his tormentor hovers over him. In another
mosaic the tormentor is shown gutting his live victim. In all the mosaics the
victims are shown to be alive as they are mutiliated and disemboweled as the the
tormentor glows with satisfaction from the pain inflicted upon the victims.
name: Sacrificial Chambers

desc:
An eerie blackness seems to flow through you as you find yourself at the heart
of the sacrificial chambers, forming a sensation of suspended animation. You can
barely feel your feet upon the floor as the black walls of the chamber seem to
engulf all light making it difficult to see the floor or the walls of the
chamber. There is no sensation of temperature or wind almost as if you,
yourself, were an essence of nothingness.
name: Heart of Sacrificial Chamber

desc:
This room has a vaguely familiar look as if you had seen it before in a picture
or engraving. Near the middle of the room the outline of a large table, some
five or six feet high and nine feet long is faintly visible in the dim light. In
the centre of the table a drawer with a golden handle reflects a faint ray of
light as it plays in the shadow. As in the previous rooms, the walls are an
eerie black making them difficult to see.
name: Sacrificial Room

desc:
The victims must have been held in this room while waiting for their turn on the
table. Lined up like sheep with their will defeated they waited for their fate.
Along the walls there are shackles, some with skeletons of the more fortunate,
who for one reason or another died before being processed.
name: Holding Room

desc:
The room is small and cramped, its walls lined with shelves, cabinets and hooks,
leaving little space to move about. On the far wall there is a small picture
showing Ungoliant approaching the tree of Laurelin, poison dripping from long
arching fangs. In the background, behind the tree, the faint outline of a shadow
can be seen crouching, almost in fear, but a wicked, gleeful smile seems to be
forming on its face.
name: Corridor Room

desc:
The chasm trail now follows the path of an ancient stream, which had cut a few
yards into the walls of the cave. A few well-worn stones remain, left from the
days of running water. A small pond which had existed in a natural bowl, had
long ago been replaced with ice. From this point on the remainder of the trail
is coated with ice.
name: Dry Stream

desc:
Having just fallen into the chasm near the bottom of the trail, you begin your
rather quick decent towards the bottom of the chasm. As you fall you can see the
long trail as it circles around the chasm. Ouch, this fall is going to bruise
you!
name: Falling into the Chasm

desc:
The closet, some three yards wide and three yards long, is filled with an
assortment of relics. Examining the relics, it is more likely that they are the
remains of items once owned by the visitors to these chambers as opposed to
being the belongings of the inhabitants. In the corner, you spy the glitter of
an untarnished sword.
name: Storage Closet

desc:
The dim forest is silent save for the occasional creaking as the ancient trees
are bent in the wind. The forest floor here has lain undisturbed for years, a
thick layer of slimy, decaying plant matter covers the ground. Oak trees
dominate the forest as far as can be seen in all directions.
name: Ancient Forest

desc:
Even when the sun reaches its zenith only a few beams of light would enter here.
As a result, moss is one of the few species of woodland plant to have taken hold
here. Around the base of every tree a thick blanket of damp, green moss can be
seen. Occasionally a lone mushroom sprouts from the ground, however, it is
difficult to recognise what variety they are and they are probably best left
well alone.
name: Dark, Humid Forest

desc:
The cave seems to extend no further than this. The darkness here is total and
permanent, outside the direct line of the cave entrance. The moist walls are
covered with twisted roots and the floor is damp and rough, giving plenty of
opportunities for creatures of the dark to hide.
name: A Deep Alcove in the Cave
